description: Specify wall or facet group names. Use of the group logic is described in Groups documentation. By default, s is assigned to the group slot 'Default'. Use the 'add' keyword to assign the group name to the next available slot and the 'remove' keyword to remove s from all slots. The optional 'facet' keyword can be given before the optional range to specify that the group name will be applied to facets. In this case, the range is applied to facets and not to walls.

parameters.name: s
parameters.description: Group name to assign or remove

parameters.name: keyword
parameters.description: Operation: 'add', 'remove', or 'slot'. Default: assign to 'Default' slot

parameters.name: facet
parameters.description: If specified, apply group name to facets instead of walls (range applies to facets)

parameters.name: range
parameters.description: Range of walls or facets to apply the group to. Default: all

keywords.name: add
keywords.description: Assign group name s to the next available slot

keywords.name: remove
keywords.description: Remove group name s from all slots

keywords.name: slot
keywords.description: Set group slot to s. Default slot: 'Default'

keywords.name: facet
keywords.description: Apply group name to facets instead of walls. When specified, range applies to facets

examples.description: Assign all walls to group 'boundary' in default slot
Assign all facets to group 'facetassembly' in default slot
Assign all walls to group 'container' in slot 'A'
Add walls with IDs 1-10 to group 'walls' in next available slot
Remove group 'oldgroup' from all slots for all walls
Assign facets at z-position 9-10 to group 'top'
Assign walls named 'foundation' to group 'layer1' in slot 'layer'

python_sdk_alternative.workaround: Wall.set_group(group_name, slot) or Facet.set_group(group_name, slot) for setting; Wall.group_remove(group_name, slot) or Facet.group_remove(group_name, slot) for removing - requires iteration over wall/facet range
